title.zh_cn: 记得去沙漠里找这颗心❤️｜约书亚树国家公园（Joshua Tree National Park）｜洛杉矶（Los Angles）

description.en: Joshua Tree National Park, right next to LA, has a startlingly realistic heart-shaped rock — great for photos and memes, though the real fun is hunting for it among the boulders.
description.zh_cn: 洛杉矶隔壁的约书亚树国家公园有一颗非常逼真的心形石，很适合拍照和表演表情包，不过最好玩的还是在石头堆里找它的过程。

title.en: Joshua trees are so boring, all they do is dance 💃｜Joshua Tree National Park, near LA
title.zh_cn: 约书亚树真的很无聊，他们只会跳舞💃｜约书亚树国家公园（Joshua Tree National Park）｜洛杉矶（Los Angeles）

description.en: Joshua Tree National Park sits right next to LA, home to a cheerful crowd of Joshua trees who apparently only know how to dance.
description.zh_cn: 约书亚树国家公园（Joshua Tree National Park），位于洛杉矶隔壁，这里有一群快乐的约书亚树，他们只会跳舞～

title.en: Six firsts in my life, all in Channel Islands National Park 😄
title.zh_cn: 人生的六个第一次，都在海峡群岛国家公园😄｜海峡群岛国家公园（Channel Islands National Park）｜洛杉矶

description.en: Channel Islands National Park is a super under-the-radar park off the coast of LA — dolphins, whales, sharks, this channel has it all. The islands even have rare foxes, earning it the nickname "North America's Galápagos." Bonus fact: the world's first offshore oil rig was built right here, so I like to call it the "Channel Islands Industrial Park."
description.zh_cn: 海峡群岛国家公园（Channel Islands National Park），是洛杉矶外海的一个超级小众国家公园，海豚🐬、鲸鱼🐳、鲨鱼🦈，这里的海峡啥都有，岛上还有稀有的小狐狸🦊，堪称北美的加拉帕戈斯群岛。更有趣的是这片海峡还分布着众多海上钻井平台，并且世界上的第一台海上石油钻井平台就诞生在这里，嗯，我愿称之为“海峡群岛工业生态园”。

title.en: Caving in California: exploring four lava tube caves with the iPhone 17｜Lava Beds National Monument
title.zh_cn: 加州探洞：带着iPhone17钻了四个火山洞！｜岩浆床国家纪念碑（Lava Beds National Monument）|加州

description.en: The iPhone 17 Pro's footage looks so much better than my old 13 — the new front-and-back dual-camera mode is great for vlogging too. It rained the whole time so the scenery wasn't much to look at, but caving doesn't care about weather. We explored four developed lava tubes in Lava Beds National Monument in Northern California: Mushpot, Skull, Merrill, and Boulevard Cave — none of them very long, just enough for a quick taste. The sound of rain was more interesting than any soundtrack, and the caves were dead quiet, so no music or editing this time.
description.zh_cn:
感觉iPhone 17 Pro 的画质比我之前的 iPhone 13 好太多了，新增的前后双摄模式也很适合拍vlog，这期发生在雨天其实没有很好看的风景，好在钻洞不吃天气，我们去的四个洞都是加州北部岩浆床国家纪念碑（Lava Beds National Monument）中进行了开发的家养的洞，分别是 Mushpot Cave, Skull Cave, Merill Cave 和 Boulevard Cave，能走的部分都不是很长，适合浅浅体验一下

雨声比任何bgm都有趣，洞里也很安静，就不加音乐和后期

title.en: Ever seen an upside-down stalactite? It's a fossilized hot spring｜Tufa at Mono Lake
title.zh_cn: 你见过倒立的钟乳石吗？是温泉的化石哦｜莫诺湖石灰华（Tufa at Mono Lake）｜加州

description.en: A visit to the South Tufa area on Mono Lake's south shore, right off Highway 395 in California. These pillars look weirdly cute, but once you learn they're "cement" columns built up from calcium carbonate spewed by underwater hot springs, they get a lot more interesting — you could call them "fossilized hot springs," or "upside-down stalactites."
description.zh_cn: 这是在加州395公路旁边的莫诺湖南岸探寻石灰话（South Tufa at Mono Lake）的记录，这些石头柱子看起来丑萌丑萌的，但是当了解到他们是水底的温泉喷涌的碳酸钙累积起来的“水泥”石柱之后，我觉得还挺有趣的，说白了这些柱子可以看成“温泉的化石”，或者说“倒立的钟乳石”。

title.en: Go shoot some so-so fall color and a few gorgeous reflections, pretend autumn visited you too｜Fall on Highway 395｜Yosemite
title.zh_cn: 你也要去拍一点潦草的秋色和一些绝美的倒影，假装自己今年也来过秋天｜康奈尔大学秋景｜加州395公路赏秋｜优胜美地

description.en: California Highway 395 is one of the state's best-known fall foliage drives, passing lakes big and small along the way. We cut through Yosemite National Park and stopped a while at Tenaya Lake, then passed Mono Lake on our way to Silver Lake in the June Lake Loop, and finally raced the sunset to Lake Sabrina near Bishop. The lakes and mountains were gorgeous — the fall color, not quite Cornell University.
description.zh_cn: 加州395公路（California Highway 395）是加州比较有名的一条赏秋公路，沿途有大大小小多个湖泊。我们在视频中穿越了优胜美地国家公园（Yosemite National Park)，在其中的特纳亚湖（Tenaya Lake）停留了一会，随后路过单调湖（Mono Lake）去了六月湖环线（June Lake Loop）里面的小湖银湖（Silver Lake），最后赶在日落之前去了毕晓普（Bishop）那边的萨布丽娜湖（Lake Sabrina）。湖景山景很棒，秋景不如康奈尔大学。

title.en: Where California's first gold nugget was found, a wilderness named after a golden city｜Coloma, the place California was really born
title.zh_cn: 你来到了发现加州第一块黄金的地方，一片冠以黄金城之名的荒野...｜加州真正诞生的地方｜科洛马（Coloma）

description.en: Coloma is a ghost town on the south side of scenic Lake Tahoe — but 150 years ago it was the destination everyone in the world was racing toward, because gold had been found in the South Fork American River running through it. California hadn't yet become a state during the Mexican-American War, and in the brief legal vacuum of that moment, gold hunters could keep whatever they dug up simply by getting here. Within a decade California's population exploded and it became a U.S. state — in a real sense, this is where California was actually born.
description.zh_cn: 科洛马（Coloma），是一座位于著名风景名胜太浩湖南侧的鬼城，但这里在一百五十年前曾经是全世界人们走线的目标，因为有人在流经这里的美国河（South Folk American River）发现了黄金。在美墨战争之际，加州尚未成立，此时的加州曾短暂出现过一段法律空缺的间隙，而正是在这个时刻发现了黄金——淘金者只需要跑到这里，挖出来的黄金就是自己的。短短十年，加州的人口暴涨，迅速变成了美国的一个州，某种程度上来说，这里是加州真正诞生的地方。

title.en: Wait, Russia had a colony in California? | Fort Ross
title.zh_cn: 俄罗斯居然殖民过加州？| 罗斯堡（Fort Ross）

description.en: Who knew Russia once colonized California? Fort Ross is a historic fort on the Sonoma coast in the SF Bay Area — once the southernmost outpost of Russian activity in the Americas.
description.zh_cn: 谁能想到俄罗斯人居然殖民过加州呢？罗斯堡（Fort Ross）是旧金山湾区 Sonoma 海岸线上的一个历史堡垒，曾经是俄罗斯人在美洲活动的最南端的据点。

title.en: Maybe you are a giant saguaro cactus?｜Saguaro National Park, Arizona
title.zh_cn: 也许你是一棵巨型仙人柱？｜巨人柱国家公园（Saguaro National Park）｜亚利桑那（Arizona）

description.en: Saguaro National Park sits just outside Tucson, Arizona — the main draw is a desert full of centuries-old giant saguaro cacti, twisted into all kinds of strange and charming shapes.
description.zh_cn: 巨人柱国家公园（Saguaro National Park），位于亚利桑那图森（Tucson, Arizona），主要的看点是一片沙漠中有着几百年历史的各种奇形怪状好看有趣的巨型仙人柱🌵

title.en: We climbed the giant dunes under snowy mountains at sunset 🌄｜Great Sand Dunes National Park, Colorado
title.zh_cn: 我们在日落时爬上了雪山下的大沙丘🌄｜大沙丘国家公园（Great Sand Dunes National Park）｜科罗拉多（Colorado）

description.en: Not much of a story here, just really big sand dunes — though finding them in Colorado does feel a bit out of place. Climbing them is genuinely exhausting, and finding a patch nobody's walked on yet is genuinely hard...
description.zh_cn: 没有什么故事，单纯就是大的大沙丘，不过出现在科罗拉多确实有点违和，爬起来是真的累，要找到一片没被人踩过的地方是真的难...

title.en: First-time hiker, a fjord pilgrimage, conquering my first ever "Chief"｜Stawamus Chief, Vancouver
title.zh_cn: 徒步小白，峡湾朝圣，征服人生第一位"酋长"的全记录！｜斯塔瓦姆斯酋长岩（Stawamus Chief）｜温哥华｜加拿大

description.en: A full record of hiking Stawamus Chief, a famous trail near Vancouver, from late April this year — also my first attempt at a really detailed vlog. Probably won't do one this thorough again: the workload doesn't really suit a part-time uploader like me, and honestly not many people have the patience to watch the whole thing, so a shorter cut is likely next time — 22 minutes is just too long. Biggest takeaway: real long-distance hiking takes a lot more prep — more cardio, some weight off, and a proper hiking backpack (the one I brought nearly killed me 😂). Food and water need real planning too — a bag of gummies and one bottle of water was way too casual. This trail is steep but at least short, or I might not have made it back...
description.zh_cn:
这是今年4月底徒步温哥华附近著名的斯塔瓦姆斯酋长岩（Stawamus Chief）的全记录，也是第一次尝试做比较翔实的记录——当然感觉之后可能很难会有这么详细的了，一是工作量不适合我这种兼职up主，二是其实感觉能有毅力看完的人不多，之后应该会出一个短篇版的，这个22分钟还是太长了....

最大的感受是要想真的去尝试长线徒步，还有很多准备要做，至少得再练练有氧减减肥，然后包也需要一个适合徒步的，这个包背上去是真要命😂；能量补给和饮用水也需要好好规划，带一包软糖和一瓶水未免过于敷衍....只能说虽然这个线陡但是不长，不然真的可能交代在那了...

title.en: Too much leftover yogurt? Use it to patch up waterfalls｜Takakkaw Falls, Mistaya Canyon & Natural Bridge｜Yoho & Banff
title.zh_cn: 酸奶喝不完可以用来修瀑布哦：塔喀可桂瀑布、迷死他呀峡谷和天然桥｜幽鹤国家公园（Yoho National Park）｜班夫国家公园（Banff）｜加拿大

description.en: Three waterfalls we explored across Yoho and Banff National Parks: Takakkaw Falls, Natural Bridge, and Mistaya Canyon. What they all have in common: water the color of yogurt — visually a lot of fun.
description.zh_cn: 这期是关于我们在幽鹤国家公园和班夫国家公园探索的三条瀑布：塔喀可桂瀑布（Takkaw Falls）、天然桥（Natural Bridge）和迷死他呀峡谷（Mistaya Canyon），他们的共同特点是，都有着酸奶一样的水，视觉效果很有趣。

title.en: Help us pick a new name for Canada's mini Kanas Lake!｜Emerald Lake, Yoho National Park
title.zh_cn: 一起来帮加拿大的迷你喀纳斯湖选一个新名字吧！｜幽鹤国家公园（Emerald Lake, Yoho National Park）｜加拿大

description.en: Emerald Lake is the most accessible lake in Canada's Yoho National Park — pretty much the only "public" one, while the rest of the truly gorgeous spots stay hidden away. Its scenery rivals Kanas Lake, yet it's stuck with about the most generic name imaginable — you'd think it was in Myanmar. Promise me: next time a beautiful lake needs a name, give it a better one. Ultramarine Lake, Turquoise Lake, Azure Lake, Jade-Sipping Lake, Bosom-of-Jade Lake — pick literally any of those instead. This episode isn't shot that well either — we actually came here earlier than the other episodes, before we'd figured out any real approach, so the ones that came after turned out a lot more polished. Still learning, let's improve together!
description.zh_cn:
翡翠湖（Emerald Lake），加拿大幽鹤国家公园（Yoho National Park）最好去的湖，也是几乎"公开"的湖（剩下的好看的地方都"养在深闺人未识"），它有着不输喀纳斯湖的美丽山水，却偏偏顶着一个烂大街的名字，不知道还以为在缅甸呢...答应我，下次给好看的湖一个更好的名字，群青湖、绿松湖、天青湖、饮玉湖、怀璧湖，随便选一个吧...

这期拍的不太行，去的时间实际上早于其他的几期，当时没有很好的准备，然后也没有思路，相比之下后面拍的几期完整度要好很多....但是，还在学习，一起进步吧！

title.en: Finally tasted glacier ice for the first time — and not even in Iceland 🧊｜Jasper National Park
title.zh_cn: 吃上了人生第一口冰川！竟然不在冰岛🧊｜贾斯珀国家公园（Jasper National Park）｜加拿大

description.en: I'd wanted to get up close to a glacier for ages. While in Banff, it turned out nearby Jasper National Park has a glacier (Athabasca Glacier) right off the highway, with a tour that lets you actually walk on it — so I booked it without a second thought. The glacier itself isn't much to look at though, and there's not a lot to do up there — they don't even let you get close to the ice towers — so consider this just checking off "tasted glacier ice for the first time." Wouldn't recommend drinking the glacier water though, no guarantee on hygiene.
description.zh_cn: 很早就一直想去近距离接触冰川，这次去班夫发现附近的贾斯珀国家公园（Jasper National Park）有一条冰川（Athabasca Glacier）离公路特别近，有旅游项目可以让你上冰川上去玩——于是果断安排！不过这个冰川的颜值不太行，然后上去也做不了太多事情，冰塔林也不让凑近看，所以只当是吃上人生第一口冰川了——还是不建议喝冰川水，卫生没有保障。

title.en: Banff in summer is so comfortable it makes you want to settle down and start a family right there...
title.zh_cn: 夏天的班夫很舒服，舒服到让人想原地结婚生娃...

description.en: First impressions of Banff National Park. Day one wasn't about the big-name hotspots — instead we hit some really peaceful spots, especially a small lake right at the start that genuinely made me feel like I should get married and bring a kid here on vacation someday.
description.zh_cn: 这期是关于加拿大班夫国家公园（Banff National Park）的第一印象，我们第一天没有去什么非常热门的打卡点，但是去了一些非常舒服的地方，尤其是一开始的那个小湖，是真的让人觉得应该趁早结个婚生个小孩再带过去休闲；视频中出现的地方包括班夫小镇本身（Banff,Alberta）、硫磺山缆车（Banff Gondola at Sulphur Mountain）、喀斯喀特瀑布（Cascade Falls）、"三姐妹"（Three Sisters Viewpoint）、蘑菇观景点（Hoodoos Viewpoint）以及那个没有名字的小湖；第二天我们才去打卡了班夫的三个最有名的湖：露易丝湖（Lake Louise）、梦莲湖（Moraine Lake）和佩托湖（Peyto Lake）。

title.en: If you like cats, you might also like "Cat Paw Lake" 🐱🐾｜Peyto Lake
title.zh_cn: 如果你喜欢猫的话，你可能也会喜欢"猫爪湖"🐱🐾 ｜佩托湖（Peyto Lake）

description.en: Peyto Lake is a famous, very Instagrammable lake in Banff National Park — hard to get down to the shore, but from up on the viewpoint it looks just like a cat's paw, which honestly makes for a great photo. Though beyond that, there's not much else to do here.
description.zh_cn: 佩托湖（Peyto Lake），是加拿大班夫国家公园（Banff National Park）的一个著名网红湖，虽然湖边很难下去，但是在高处看上去就像是一只"猫爪"，可以说非常出片了——尽管除此之外，也没法干啥。

title.en: Izu has no dancing girls, but Mt Fuji can dance for you 💃｜Mount Omuro, Izu
title.zh_cn: 伊豆没有舞女，但富士山可以为你跳舞💃｜大室山｜伊豆｜日本

description.en: Mount Omuro is the Izu Peninsula's famous "matcha cake" mountain — every year they hold a controlled burn ceremony that turns the matcha into chocolate for a day. Great location for a small volcanic cone, and the view of Mt Fuji from up top is excellent too — the kind of place I'd want to visit often if I lived in Tokyo. Too bad I don't. I'd heard of Izu through Kawabata Yasunari's "The Dancing Girl of Izu," so when Old Liu suggested going to Izu I did jokingly ask if there were any dancing girls there — though honestly the novel has little to do with Izu itself, just a fun reference.
description.zh_cn:
大室山，伊豆半岛著名的抹茶蛋糕山，每年都会有烧山仪式把抹茶换成巧克力，位置很不错，作为一个小火山锥很棒，在山上眺望富士山也很好，属于我如果住在东京会想经常来的地方——只是可惜我不住在东京。

听说伊豆是因为川端康成的《伊豆的舞女》，所以在老刘说去伊豆的时候我的确开玩笑地问伊豆有没有舞女，不过其实《伊豆的舞女》跟伊豆关系不大，只是好玩罢了哈哈。

title.en: Next time you visit Izu Panorama Park, remember to bring love with you
title.zh_cn: 下次去伊豆全景公园的时候，记得带上爱情｜伊豆半岛｜日本

description.en: Just a plain record of Izu Panorama Park — couldn't find a fitting soundtrack for it (kind of like not being able to find love), so I just skipped music entirely. If this place is worth visiting at all, it's probably only in autumn — the ¥2500 ticket felt a bit steep otherwise, and I doubt many people are dying to go, so consider this purely a travel log.
description.zh_cn: 伊豆全景公园的流水账，因为找不到合适的背景音乐（就像找不到爱情一样）干脆就不放了，反正这个地方说值得去的话目测也只有秋天值得，其他时候2500日元的票感觉有点贵，估计也没啥人想去想看，纯当旅行记录了

title.en: I don't want to be human anymore, I want to be a swan at the foot of Mt Fuji｜Hirano-no-hama, Lake Yamanaka
title.zh_cn: 我不想当人了，我想在富士山脚下当一只天鹅｜平野之滨｜山中湖｜日本

description.en: Feeding swans at Hirano-no-hama on Lake Yamanaka is absolutely the best way to experience Mt Fuji — if I could, I'd want to be a swan at the foot of Mt Fuji at Hirano-no-hama too.
description.zh_cn: 在富士山山中湖平野之滨喂天鹅绝对是打开富士山的最佳方式，如果可以，我也想在平野之滨做一只富士山脚下的天鹅
